rulings.Case Title: ESPERANZA COTA ROMERO VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Standing is not limited to those persons who have the statutory right to control disposition of the remains. (Christensen v. Sup.Ct. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 868, 875.) For the first cause of action, there are triable issues of fact whether UCSF maintained the applicable standard of care in performing the autopsy as proffered by the declarations of Defendant's expert Dr. Rosenthal and Plaintiffs' expert Dr. Bronston. (Flowers v. Torrance Mem'l Hosp. Med. Ctr. (1994) 8 Cal.4th 992, 1001; Kelley v. Trunk (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 519, 524.) Further, there are triable issues of fact whether fraud, overreaching or excusable neglect to excuse Esperanza Cota Romero's execution of the Autopsy Permit without first reading the instrument before signing it. (Stewart v. Preston Pipeline Inc. (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1565, 1588.) For the second cause of action, Defendant has not met its initial burden to demonstrate that as a public entity, it is immune from liability based on a cause of action for fraud. (Gov. Code § 818.8; 5 Witkin, Cal. Summary (10th ed. 2005) Torts, § 242, p. 398.) Plaintiffs' motion to strike Dr. Rosenthal's declaration is denied. The objections are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL ANN LEVINE VS. PETER N. HADIARIS ESQ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT PETER HADIARIS' Motion To Compel Arbitration. Granted. The unambiguous language in the Attorney Client Agreement provides that malpractice disputes are subject to binding arbitration. Defendant has not waived his right to enforce the arbitration clause. Plaintiff has not established that the Agreement or enforcement of the arbitration clause is unconscionable. Plaintiff was notified of General Conditions at the time she executed the Agreement. (See Agreement, 4, "This agreement is subject to the general provisions which appear on the reverse or accompany this agreement.") Plaintiff was informed of Defendant's intent to arbitrate by his initial Case Management Statement. (See Case Management Statement, filed July 31, 2013, 15.) Plaintiff's objection to new matters raised in Defendant's reply is overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LEONID GONCHAROV et al VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court grants defendant's request for judicial notice. The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to the UCL, accounting, and declaratory relief causes of action. Judicial abstention is appropriate because the state and local agencies are better situated to address the novel issues regarding defendant's business. The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to the interference causes of action because plaintiff pleads no facts showing an existing relationship with future speculative cab riders. See Roth v. Rhodes (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 530, 546. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRAYTON PURCELL LLP et al VS. DAVID J. COOK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 6, 2013, Line 4: Cross Complainants DAVID COOK'S and COOK COLLECTION ATTORNEYS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Philip Anderson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. The proposed order by moving party Cook will be signed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT DET NORSKE VERITAS (U.S.A.) INC. and DET NORSKE VERITAS' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted. Defendants have met their initial burden to demonstrate that the eighth cause of action of Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint arises from Defendant's constitutionally-protected free speech or petition activity. (Equilon Enterprises, LLC v. Consumer Cause, Inc. (2002( 29 Cal.4th 53, 61.) Here, Plaintiff alleges, inter alia, that Defendants negligently supervised and retained Defendant Rescalvo by permitting him to file a cross-complaint in the instant action. (SAC, 145.) Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the merits. In fact, Plaintiff has deleted these allegations in his Third Amended Complaint. However, "Plaintiff cannot avoid an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the complaint prior to the hearing on the motion." (Weil & Brown, 7:1121. See Sylmar Air Conditioning v. Pueblo Contracting Services, Inc. (2004) 122 Cal.App.4th 1049, 1054.) The Court does not consider the evidence proffered for the first time in reply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHI WONG et al VS. BILL WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Opposition Due 10/30; Reply Due 11/04)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2013, Line 16, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. The Court denies plaintiff's application for a preliminary injunction. Plaintiff does not plead a cognizable cause of action. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on the merits and the Court finds defendant's version of the by-laws to be more persuasive. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate irreparable harm or explain why they did not seek relief sooner given that the alleged removal occurred in August of 2013.
